====Pokémon====
This listing is of Steven's Pokémon in the [[Pokémon Adventures]] manga.
{{anchor|Metagross 2}}{{TrainerPoké
|main=Steven's Metagross
|trainer=Steven
|pkmn=Metagross
|mega=yes
|type1=Steel
|type2=Psychic
|img=Steven Metagross Adventures.png
|cap=Steven's Metagross
|img2=Steven Mega Metagross Adventures.png
|cap2=Steven's Mega Metagross
|epnum=PS191
|epname=Blowing Past Nosepass II
|desc={{p|Metagross}} is Steven's main Pokémon and strongest as well. It is often used as a method of flight transportation. In the {{MangaArc|Omega Ruby & Alpha Sapphire}}, it was given the ability to Mega Evolve into Mega Metagross.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Steven
|pkmn=Beldum (multiple)
|type1=Steel
|type2=Psychic
|img=Steven Beldum Adventures.png
|cap=Steven's Beldum
|epnum=PS196
|epname=Ring Ring Goes Beldum
|desc=Steven is seen with four {{p|Beldum}}. He first used them in Granite Cave to stop a group of {{p|Mawile}} from attacking Ruby. They did so by coming out of some rocks. These Beldum later lined up with Sapphire's [[Sapphire (Adventures)#At Sea|Lorry and Relly]] to unseal the [[legendary giants]]. One of his Beldum was also seen when Steven signed a contract to sell his Villa.

Another one of his Beldum, nicknamed '''Iron''' (Japanese: こうてつ ''Kōtetsu''), was seen with Steven in the {{MangaArc|Omega Ruby & Alpha Sapphire}}. It is based on the [[#Traded to the player|Beldum]] the player receives from Steven in a [[In-game trade#HeartGold and SoulSilver|trade]] in {{game|HeartGold and SoulSilver|s}}.

Their only known move is {{m|Take Down}}{{tt|*|revealed on an official site only}}, and their Ability is {{a|Clear Body}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Steven
|pkmn=Metang (multiple)
|type1=Steel
|type2=Psychic
|img=Steven Metang Adventures.png
|cap=Steven's Metang
|epnum=PS196
|epname=Ring Ring Goes Beldum
|desc=Steven owns multiple {{p|Metang}}. They were first used to stop a group of Mawile in Granite Cave. Steven lent his Metang to the Elite Four so that they would be able to stay in the air while they tried to contain Groudon and Kyogre's power.

None of Metang's moves are known, and its Ability is {{a|Clear Body}}.}}

=====Temporary=====
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Steven
|pkmn=Registeel
|type1=Steel
|img=Brandon Registeel Adventures.png
|epnum=PS254
|epname=A Royal Rumble with Regirock, Regice and Registeel II
|main=Brandon's legendary giants
|main_section=Pokémon Adventures
|desc={{p|Registeel}} was awakened by Steven and the Elite Four to be used to contain Groudon and Kyogre's power. Steven controlled Registeel with Wallace while the others controlled Regice and Regirock. After Steven died from the strain of controlling the three Pokémon, Registeel lashed out and ran away. It was later found by [[Brandon]] and captured.}}

=====Borrowed=====
{{TrainerPoké
|main=Mumu
|trainer=Ruby
|pkmn=Swampert
|nick=Mumu
|gender=male
|type1=Water
|type2=Ground
|img=Mumu.png
|epnum=PS183
|epname=Trying to Trounce Torchic
|desc={{p|Swampert|Mumu}} was lent to Steven while Ruby was away on [[Southern Island]]. While under Steven's care, he trained to fully master {{m|Hydro Cannon}} to prepare for his Mega Evolution.}}
